content.value: Basics of Identifying Networks

Understanding which network a container is connected to is essential for troubleshooting connectivity issues and ensuring security isolation. Docker provides several built-in commands to query the network state of both containers and the host system.
How to Identify Which Network Mode is Being Used by a Container
Docker provides commands to check the network attached to a container. A user can identify whether the container uses **bridge, host, none, or custom network mode**.
1. docker inspect <container>
Purpose: Used to display detailed information about a container, including its networking settings, IP addresses, and gateway configuration.
docker inspect <container_name>
content.label: Syntax

content.value: What to Check? Look for the "NetworkSettings" section. Inside it, you will find "Networks". If "bridge" appears, the container uses Bridge Network Mode.
Student Understanding: Think of it as checking the container's network identity card.
2. docker network ls
Purpose: Lists all Docker networks available in the system.
docker network ls
content.label: Syntax

content.value: Meaning: This command helps you know what 'roads' (networks) exist in Docker. Default networks include bridge, host, and none.
Student Understanding: Think of this as showing all available roads for containers to travel.
3. docker network inspect <network_name>
Purpose: Shows detailed information about a specific network, including all containers currently connected to it.
docker network inspect <network_name>
content.label: Syntax

content.value: What It Shows: Connected containers, network type, IP range, gateway, and configuration details.
Student Understanding: Think of it as checking the details of one particular road.
Interactive Simulation: The Discovery Flow
Follow the flow below to see how these commands work together to identify and explore container networks.
One-Line Exam Definition

A user can identify the network mode of a container using Docker commands such as docker inspect, docker network ls, and docker network inspect. These commands display network details and connected container information.

content.title: Show how a user can identify which network mode is being used by a container.

contentBlocks.value: To identify which network mode is being used by a Docker container, you can use the following commands:

items:
1. Inspect the container:
docker inspect <container_name/id>
This command provides detailed information about the container, including its network settings.

2. List all networks:
docker network ls
This command lists all the networks available on the Docker host.

3. Inspect a specific network:
docker network inspect <network_name>
This command provides detailed information about a specific network, including which containers are connected to it.
